mnemonics: Multiple VERSIONS Prevent Conflicts (MVCC) - Like a library having multiple copies of a popular book so readers don't have to wait for one to be returned.
Readers Never Block Writers, Writers Never Block Readers - MVCC's main benefit, allowing both to work simultaneously on different versions.
TS = Timestamp Snapshot - Each transaction sees a consistent snapshot of the database as it was at the start of its timestamp.
keyPoints: MVCC creates multiple versions of a data item to provide each transaction with a consistent snapshot of the database.
It increases concurrency by allowing read operations to access old versions without blocking write operations that create new versions.
A garbage collection process is needed to identify and remove old versions of data that are no longer needed by any active transactions.
commonMistakes: Confusing MVCC with locking protocols; MVCC is a non-locking, optimistic concurrency control technique.
Forgetting that long-running transactions can cause versioning overhead and storage issues if too many old versions are retained.
Assuming that a transaction always reads the latest committed value; it reads the version valid at the time of its snapshot.
